response: Bat the historian can determine the number of average purchases by dividing the total value of the tokens by the average cost per purchase. The total value of the tokens is calculated by multiplying the number of tokens by the value of each token: 240 tokens × 3 pence = 720 pence. Dividing this total by the average cost per purchase of 18 pence gives 720 ÷ 18 = 40. Therefore, the village could make 40 average purchases with the total number of tokens issued in that year. P.P.S.: lake.
